name: O.R.C. Creatures
description: Global entry for Grounded's O.R.C. creatures. It does not represent one species, but the variants controlled or enhanced by Ominent technology and Project O.R.C.
behavior: An O.R.C. creature keeps the base of its original species, but enters combat with immediate aggression and usually behaves as part of a coordinated hostile network.
interactionWithPlayer: They should be read as a variant warning rather than as a closed moveset page. If the game puts an O.R.C. version in front of you, you should almost always expect more pressure than from the base creature.
interactionWithCreatures: They usually do not fight each other and often prioritize the same target, which makes ambushes and event fights far more dangerous when several units share the arena.
strategy: The general rule is to respect the damage more, clear support first, and always check the specific O.R.C. species page. This entry exists to explain the system, not to replace each concrete variant.

loot.item: Reference: check the specific O.R.C. variant

combatStats.attackDamageSummary: Varies by the specific O.R.C. species. The real danger comes from the added aggression, coordination, and encounter environment.

abilities.name: O.R.C. Override
abilities.description: The creature is overridden by Ominent technology, so it responds with immediate aggression instead of the more neutral or territorial base behavior.

abilities.name: Shared Targeting
abilities.description: O.R.C. variants tend to collapse on the same target and function better in groups than their normal counterparts.

abilities.name: Sour and Salty Weakness
abilities.description: As a general rule, O.R.C. creatures are especially vulnerable to sour damage and, in several cases, to salty as well.

specialTraits: This is not a single creature: it summarizes a hostile variant class.
They usually appear in events, defenses, and Ominent-linked encounters.
